methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $25M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 8 private companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation; no public comparables exist in this narrowly defined segment.

Given the early-stage nature and heterogeneity of the peer set, a single valuation method was applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-subsequent-round progression, yielding an implied valuation of $50M. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while four same-sector peers exist, none satisfied minimum clustering thresholds (three or more datapoints in same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), making sector-wide medians unreliably broad.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M, supported by the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include (1) the representativeness of historical stage-progression multiples for this specific subsector, given limited comparable transactions; (2) the wide dispersion and varied business models among existing Drones & Autonomous peers; and (3) Rainmaker's own unit economics and path to revenue, which the provided data does not address. Additional diligence on customer traction and capital efficiency would strengthen conviction within this range.
